lead: Recent developments in health technology and policy highlight the evolving landscape of healthcare, emphasizing the importance of real-world evidence, mental health impacts, and the integration of new health applications.

stories.title: Why the FDA’s new real-world evidence guidance ends the era of structured-data-only submissions
stories.summary: The FDA's new guidance on real-world evidence marks a significant shift in regulatory submissions for medical devices, emphasizing the need for accurate and reliable data. It highlights the limitations of structured data in capturing critical clinical information, particularly in areas like social determinants of health and family history. This change necessitates a comprehensive approach to data collection and analysis, integrating unstructured data to ensure regulatory submissions reflect true patient outcomes.

stories.title: You Can Now Use Your HSA/FSA Funds to Buy a Fitbit Air
stories.summary: Health savings accounts (HSA) and flexible spending accounts (FSA) can now be used to purchase the new Fitbit Air, making fitness tracking more accessible. This change allows consumers to leverage pre-tax dollars for health-related purchases, promoting a proactive approach to personal health management. The integration of fitness technology into health financing reflects a growing trend towards preventative health measures.

stories.title: ‘We could make a lot of money’: The health app saying no to peptides
stories.summary: Everlab, a health app valued at nearly $500 million, is focusing on preventative health while rejecting the use of peptides, despite their popularity. Backed by a prominent cricketer, the app aims to mainstream preventative health strategies, although the scientific backing for such approaches is still developing. This highlights the tension between emerging health trends and established medical practices.

stories.title: Online racism is significantly affecting mental health, First Nations people say: ‘It’s like carrying a bully in your pocket’
stories.summary: The Australian Human Rights Commission has called for a digital duty of care to combat the negative mental health impacts of online racism on First Nations people. Social media algorithms are criticized for promoting racist content, exacerbating mental health challenges within these communities. This issue underscores the urgent need for responsible digital practices to protect vulnerable populations.

stories.title: I tried Google’s new health app. It can’t replace a real trainer
stories.summary: A recent trial of Google's new health app reveals its limitations in providing personalized training compared to human trainers. While the app offers insights into heart health, sleep, and exercise, it lacks the nuanced guidance that a real trainer can provide. This highlights the ongoing challenge of integrating technology into personalized health and fitness regimens.
